matches.rationale: The Bash rule is triggered when creating a pull request after modifying a library and requires consulting the library pre-command instruction. This is the same pre-PR action context as running make format, although the extracted evidence omits the actual make format command (and the sibling lint/test commands); under the partial-coverage rule it is assigned to this single closest gold rule only.

The Edit rule targets double-backtick inline-code text in Python source, and its Korean evidence explicitly prohibits Sphinx-style double backticks in docstrings and comments, matching the gold trigger.

misses.note: The compiler collapsed the shared pre-PR list into lib-pr-precommands. That extracted rule is already consumed by g1 under the 1:1 constraint, and there is no separate rule targeting make lint.
The compiler collapsed the shared pre-PR list into lib-pr-precommands. That extracted rule is already consumed by g1 under the 1:1 constraint, and there is no separate rule targeting make test.
No extracted Bash rule targets setting the TEST variable to select a test file or pass pytest options.

The single-backtick requirement was merged into no-sphinx-double-backtick's evidence. That extracted rule is already consumed by the closer prohibition rule g5 under the 1:1 constraint, leaving no separate Edit rule for using single backticks.
